purpose: JUCE application entry point. Defines PluginHostApp (subclass of juce::JUCEApplication) which on initialise() sets up ApplicationProperties, a date-stamped FileLogger, the default LookAndFeel, and constructs the single IconMenu instance. Owns appProperties and fileLogger for the rest of the process lifetime; exposes getAppProperties() free function consumed by IconMenu.
invariants: Application name 'Light Host' is the source of truth — used by getApplicationName(), the PropertiesFile suffix, the FileLogger prefix, and propagated to JUCE_APPLICATION_NAME_STRING via CMake.
JUCE_PLUGINHOST_VST3 || JUCE_PLUGINHOST_AU must be 1 — guarded by a #error at file top; CMake currently enables all three (VST/VST3/AU).

iconMenu is constructed in initialise() and reset in shutdown(). It must not be constructed before applyMultiInstanceSuffix and the FileLogger setup — IconMenu's constructor immediately reads getAppProperties() and writes to the logger.

Multi-instance mode: command-line flag '-multi-instance=<name>' makes moreThanOneInstanceAllowed() return true AND prefixes the settings filename with <name>., so each instance has its own persisted state.
macOS hides the Dock icon (setDockIconVisible(false)) so the app is menu-bar-only; on Windows the app is tray-only and there is no equivalent call.
gotchas: getAppProperties() does a dynamic_cast<PluginHostApp*> on JUCEApplication::getInstance() — calling it before initialise() or after shutdown() will crash. IconMenu must never store a stale reference.

FileLogger is created lazily-on-the-spot in initialise() and explicitly cleared (setCurrentLogger(nullptr)) before fileLogger.reset() in shutdown(). Reordering these lines causes use-after-free during teardown.
Multi-instance suffix only takes effect when the command-line flag is present at launch; switching instances at runtime is not supported.
[[maybe_unused]] on commandLine is intentional — JUCE delivers it, we only use getCommandLineParameterArray() instead because it handles quoting/escaping for us.
